task: Define and ship sitemap inclusion policy for indexable trust pages
current_evidence: Live sitemap had 96 URLs and did not include privacy, terms, support, or method-boundaries pages even though those pages were reachable and indexable.
expected_change: Core trust pages are included in sitemap only when they return 200, indexable metadata, canonical self or approved locale canonical, and CMS or backend authority exists.
acceptance_criteria: Sitemap contains every approved trust URL; each listed URL returns 200; robots meta is index or omitted; canonical matches the approved URL; no frontend body copy is introduced.
notes: This is a discovery and trust foundation task, not a content-writing task.

task: Resolve clinical and depression page indexability versus sitemap mismatch
current_evidence: Clinical and depression test pages were indexable in live page metadata and API lookup, but were absent from the live sitemap.
expected_change: A documented decision exists for each high-risk page: either indexable plus sitemap only after review_completed=true, or noindex plus no sitemap. The selected behavior is consistent across zh and en.
acceptance_criteria: Decision record is approved by product, SEO, and content review; noindex or pending pages are absent from sitemap and footer/global nav; indexable pages have review_completed=true before sitemap inclusion.
notes: Do not optimize these pages with aggressive commercial claims.

task: Add footer trust links only to approved existing pages
current_evidence: SiteFooter policyLinks was empty; live footer did not expose privacy, terms, support, or method boundary links.
expected_change: Footer exposes stable trust links for privacy, terms, support/help, method boundaries, and approved core tests from the P0 allowlist. Holdlist pages remain hidden.
acceptance_criteria: Footer has no 404 links; privacy, terms, support, and method-boundaries are reachable from all public pages; refund, science, methodology, reliability, results, and business subpages are not linked in P0.
notes: Footer link labels can be product UI strings, but page body content must remain CMS/backend-authoritative.

task: Remove or resolve English trust-layer 404s before linking them globally
current_evidence: English versions of charter, brand, foundation, careers, and policies returned 404, while Chinese versions existed.
expected_change: Each English trust page is either created through CMS/content_pages with metadata and review, or excluded from footer, sitemap, and hreflang alternates until content exists.
acceptance_criteria: No global navigation, footer, sitemap, or hreflang entry points to these English URLs unless the URL returns 200 with approved CMS content and metadata.
notes: This task is about eliminating broken trust paths, not translating all company content in frontend.

task: Decide and fix bare /help canonical behavior
current_evidence: Localized /help redirected to support, but bare /help returned 404.
expected_change: Bare /help no longer returns 404 and uses deterministic redirects to the existing support canonical paths.
acceptance_criteria: Required redirects are deterministic: /help -> /zh/support, /zh/help -> /zh/support, /en/help -> /en/support; sitemap uses only /zh/support and /en/support.
notes: Short-term recommendation is to keep /support as canonical. Do not use Accept-Language as the SEO critical path.

task: Decide results hub versus private noindex results lookup flow
current_evidence: /results redirected to /results/lookup, and lookup was noindex/noarchive/nofollow, blocking a future public result interpretation hub.
expected_change: P0 reserves /results for a future public hub but does not publish result-guide content. /results/lookup remains a private noindex utility route.
acceptance_criteria: /results/lookup remains noindex and absent from sitemap; /results is not linked from footer/global nav until P1 CMS/backend result-guide authority exists; no frontend result-guide body copy is added.
notes: This is a route decision or reservation task only. Public results hub publication is P1.

task: Prepare refund-policy CMS ticket and keep refund-policy off global links
current_evidence: /refund and localized refund URLs redirected to support; refund content page API returned 404 in audit.
expected_change: Refund-policy stays on the P0 holdlist. A P1 CMS/legal ticket is created only after business confirmation; no global footer/nav link points to refund-policy in P0.
acceptance_criteria: dependency=business_confirmation; refund-policy is not linked from footer/global nav during P0; P1 publication requires approved refund SLA and eligibility rules.
notes: Downgraded because refund-policy publication is content/legal work, not a P0 foundation fix.

task: Define root policy URL redirects for /privacy and /terms
current_evidence: Root /privacy and /terms returned 404 while localized privacy and terms existed.
expected_change: Root policy URLs use deterministic redirects to Chinese default policy pages instead of returning 404.
acceptance_criteria: /privacy -> /zh/privacy and /terms -> /zh/terms with permanent redirects; localized pages remain indexable and listed in sitemap.
notes: P0 does not use Accept-Language as the SEO critical path.

task: Resolve /career/jobs sitemap inclusion versus indexable page state
current_evidence: Career jobs pages were indexable but absent from sitemap in the audit.
expected_change: Career jobs are either included in sitemap after performance and content-authority readiness, or marked noindex until readiness is confirmed.
acceptance_criteria: Decision is documented; robots/index metadata and sitemap inclusion match; no orphan indexable career job hub remains outside sitemap.
notes: Treat this as sitemap policy consistency, not a career content expansion task.

task: Create a no-link-until-content-exists gate for new trust and science pages
current_evidence: Several proposed trust, science, and result pages do not yet exist or lack CMS authority.
expected_change: Navigation, footer, sitemap, hreflang, and article/test internal links include only URLs with CMS/backend authority and approved metadata.
acceptance_criteria: A release checklist blocks links to 404, draft, noindex, or missing-CMS pages; all approved links are validated by status, canonical, and robots checks before release.
notes: This prevents SEO plans from creating new broken routes.

task: Document canonical URL architecture for locale, help, policy, and result routes
current_evidence: The audit found mixed but mostly workable patterns: /zh redirects to root for homepage, localized inner pages exist, bare help and root policy routes were inconsistent.
expected_change: A written decision specifies canonical behavior for home, locale-prefixed inner pages, root utility paths, support/help, and public/private results routes.
acceptance_criteria: The site-map proposal contains explicit route rules, redirect rules, sitemap inclusion rules, and pages that must not be linked until content exists.
notes: This is the planning control that prevents fragmented implementation.

task: Define high-risk page review gate for clinical, depression, anxiety, and IQ URLs
current_evidence: The report identified clinical/depression and IQ pages as high-risk surfaces requiring boundary language and review.
expected_change: High-risk pages require content review before indexable launch, footer linking, sitemap inclusion, or paid-report upsell messaging.
acceptance_criteria: Review_required is true in page backlog for clinical and IQ pages; acceptance check confirms disclaimer, professional-help boundary, non-diagnostic language, and no unverified accuracy claims.
notes: Review can be product/legal/clinical depending on available staff, but missing reviewer must be marked as needs manual confirmation.

task: Validate sitemap, footer, and 404 fixes with a route-level SEO smoke matrix
affected_files_or_urls: /sitemap.xml; /robots.txt; footer links; trust pages; high-risk test pages; /help; /results
current_evidence: The audit relied on route samples and identified route mismatches that need regression protection.
expected_change: A reusable validation matrix checks status code, indexability, canonical, sitemap presence, footer presence, and no 404 links for P0 routes.
acceptance_criteria: All P0 URLs have recorded status, canonical, robots/index state, sitemap state, and internal-link source; any failure blocks release until fixed or explicitly deferred.
notes: This can be a spreadsheet or docs checklist; no business code is required for the planning deliverable.
